title: Supplement
description: A supplementary creative work associated with a document.
$comment:
This is a Stencila-native type corresponding closely to the JATS
[`<supplementary-material>`](https://jats.nlm.nih.gov/archiving/tag-library/1.1/element/supplementary-material.html)
element.

It is used to attach supplementary works to a document while still allowing
them to appear as block content near the material they support, such as within
a figure caption or a dedicated supplementary materials section. This lets
Stencila model supplements as document content rather than only as distant
metadata attachments.

Key properties include `workType`, `label`, `caption`, and `target`.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

idAutomatically.description: Whether the identifier should be automatically updated.
idAutomatically.$comment:
When true, the `id` has been generated from the node's label or other
document context and should not be encoded to author-facing source formats
as a persistent identifier.

label.description: A short label for the node.

labelAutomatically.description: Whether the label should be automatically updated.

caption.description: A brief caption or description for the supplement.
caption.$comment:
Supplements that are figures or tables often include their own 
detailed captions. This property is intended for a shorter, 
general description of the supplementary material.

target.$comment: Usually a relative path but may be a full URL.

compilationMessages.description: Any messages generated while embedding the supplement.

work.$comment:
Often only a reference to the supplementary work (i.e., just `target`) will be available. 
However, this property also allows inclusion of the full content of the work if necessary.
